title: Command for fetching machine resource usage
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I am managing a podman machine, and would like an easy way of querying podman for _machine_ runtime statistics (as opposed to container) such as CPU and memory usage which would then query the underlying virtualization engine. This is not the same as `podman stats`, as there is a nonzero RAM usage from the machine itself that is not accounted for when summing up the values from the containers running on it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run `podman machine inspect`
2. notice that the resources block only lists the _maximum_ values
3. wonder, hey, wouldn't it be cool if you could see the current values?

**Output of `podman version`

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 15:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body: In the mean time, it is enough for me to use the ssh sock and pull out the data from the fedora vm, as it is the only cross platform way of doing it that I've managed to find.
@baude Didn't you add this to machine info?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
it is a fair idea and fair request ... i have a bunch of unanswered questions that will take some time to evaluate.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A good idea, not opposed to it at all, but definitely needs more research to take a look at how this could be done.
@arlyon you are the reporter, what would you like to see?

Essentially just an easy way to see current VM resource consumption, rather than just the maximum allocated amount. It is handy to have that if you are running close to max and just want to do a sanity check over RAM consumption etc.

Is this still wanted?

podman info already shows
```
  cpuUtilization:
    idlePercent: 92.44
    systemPercent: 1.12
    userPercent: 6.44
  cpus: 12

```
nothing for memory though.
The problem with podman machine inspect would be that we would actually have to query inside the VM which AFAIK we do not do right now as we just display the host side.
